w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: LAGOS 2058 is a 12-turn political campaign simulation set in a fictional 2058 Nigeria. Players control political parties competing for electoral dominance across 774 Local Government Areas (LGAs) grouped into 8 Administrative Zones (AZs).
Victory is determined by popular vote share and seat allocation. Each LGA awards one seat (774 total) to the party with the plurality of votes. The party with the most seats wins.

The game uses a Merrill-Grofman spatial voting model. Every voter and every party has a position on 28 issue dimensions (ranging from -5 to +5). Voters prefer parties whose positions are close to their own. Campaign actions shift which issues voters care about, how well-known your party is, and your general appeal.
Political Capital is the currency of campaigning. Every action costs PC. Manage it wisely.
Base income: 7 PC per turn (received at start of each turn)
ETO dividend: +1 PC if any Economic ETO score is 7 or higher (max +2/turn)
Fundraising: generates additional PC (see Fundraising action)
You may hold a maximum of 18 PC at the start of any turn. Excess PC above this cap is lost before income is applied. Spend it or lose it.
There is no limit on the number of actions per turn. You may take as many actions as you can afford. Only your PC balance constrains you.
There are 14 distinct action types. Each action has a base PC cost, a target scope, and effects across one or more campaign channels.
Some actions cost more when targeting
or spending more for increased impact.
A public rally in a target district. Core campaign activity.
Raises salience on language-profiled issue dimensions
Different languages emphasize different issues at rallies:
Hausa: Sharia, fertility policy, education, traditional authority
Yoruba: fiscal autonomy, resource revenue, AZ restructuring
Igbo: fiscal autonomy, AZ restructuring, trade policy
Pidgin: housing, taxation, labor automation, healthcare
Broad messaging across Administrative Zones via media channels.
Medium options: radio (rural reach), TV (urban reach, +1 PC), social media (internet-connected)
Raises salience and awareness across targeted zones
A major national policy statement that repositions your party on the issues.
REQUIRED by week three of the game
Updates your party's position vector on the 28 issue dimensions
Raises awareness nationally (proportional to media infrastructure)
May be done more than once if a party wishes to dramatically realign itself.
Sharp reversals (>3-point shift on any dimension) trigger:
Door-to-door canvassing and local mobilization in target districts.
Secure backing from influential figures. Effect depends on endorser type.
30% chance endorsers withdraw if you use ethnic mobilization in the same region.
Endorsers and identity politics don't mix well.
Identity-targeted mobilization appealing to ethnic solidarity.
Boosts salience on identity dimensions (ethnic quotas, traditional authority, constitutional structure, AZ restructuring)
Awareness boost scaled by ethnic population percentage in region
Distributing resources to build local loyalty networks.
Patronage networks mobilize voters effectively but accumulate scandal risk.
Investigate and publicize an opponent's weaknesses.
Raises salience on dimensions where the target party is weak
Applies -0.08 valence to the target party
Minor awareness boost for your party (+0.02)
The cheapest and most volatile action. Press conferences, interviews, social media blitzes.
Activates all channels: salience, valence, awareness
Tone options: positive (default), negative (attacks opponent), contrast (amplified salience)
impact scaled by 2x deviation from score 5
Build institutional support with Elite, Economic, Labor, or Youth organizations
O score (0-10) in the target zone
Awareness boost per point of score change
ETO pays off over time: scores of 7+ grant +1 PC/turn dividend.
Scores of 5+ unlock free ETO Intelligence actions.
Parties that ignore ETO suffer PC shortages in turns 9-12.
React to negative events to protect your reputation.
Spend PC to generate more PC. Source determines yield and side effects.
Consecutive penalty: using the same source repeatedly costs -1 PC yield per repeat
Economic ETO bonus: if Economic ETO score is 7+, any fundraising gets +1 PC
Quality scaling: poor quality can result in net loss; excellent quality boosts yield
Commission polling to gather intelligence on voter positions.
Poll results are delivered next turn, not immediately.
zone-level voter position data. Requires ETO score of 5 or higher in the target zone.
Every campaign action operates through one or more of these five channels. Understanding them is key to effective strategy.
How well-known your party is in each LGA. Ranges from 0.60 (floor) to 1.0 (cap).
more aware of what your party’s positions actually are
Most actions contribute some awareness boost
How much voters in a region care about each of the 28 issue dimensions.
Base salience varies by LGA (regional and ethnic factors)
General party attractiveness independent of ideology. Think of it as likability or trustworthiness.
Boosted by: endorsements, ground game, positive media, crisis response
Damaged by: opposition research (from opponents), scandals, manifesto reversals, high exposure
Directly added to voter utility calculation
The maximum turnout achievable in an LGA, acting as a soft cap above the baseline.
Ground game and heavy advertising raise the ceiling
Adjusts the baseline abstention utility. Negative values encourage turnout; positive values suppress it.
In close races, turnout decides seats. Ground game is the primary turnout driver.
Patronage also drives turnout effectively, but carries exposure risk.
A party with great policy positions but low turnout will lose to a weaker party that gets voters to the polls.
Cohesion represents your party's internal unity on a scale of 0 to 10. All parties start at 10 (perfect unity). Lower cohesion weakens every campaign action.
Your cohesion level determines an effectiveness multiplier applied to all campaign effects:
The game tracks which AZs you campaign in each turn
Neglect penalty: if your support base AZ is not engaged for 3+ consecutive turns, you lose -1.0 cohesion
Engagement bonus: campaigning in 3+ distinct AZs in a single turn grants +1.0 cohesion
Exposure is cumulative reputational risk from controversial actions. Too much exposure triggers scandals.
Each turn, the game rolls for scandal based on your current exposure:
When a scandal triggers, your party suffers:
Exposure halved (partial reputation recovery after the fallout)
After 3 consecutive clean turns (no new exposure), exposure decays by -1.0 per turn
valence per point of exposure above 1.0
Front-load risky actions (ethnic mobilization, patronage) early in the campaign.
After turn 6, switch to clean actions to let exposure decay.
A late-game scandal is devastating: PC loss + valence hit + cohesion damage all at once.
Every action receives a quality score from the Game Master that determines its effectiveness. Higher quality means stronger effects.
GM Score = Strategic Fit (1-5) + Quality (1-5), giving a range of 2-10.
Is this the right action for the right region, demographic, issue, and language?
How detailed, realistic, and contextually aware is the action?
Each of the following adds +1 to quality (capped at 5 total):
Providing actual speech content, ad copy, or narrative
Describing visual or audio production elements
The final score is converted to an effectiveness multiplier:
ETO Interaction: +1 if any ETO score is 7+
Low Cohesion: -1 if cohesion is between 4-5
Media Volatility: 2x deviation from score 5 (amplifies both good and bad)
Poll and ETO Intelligence actions always use a 1.0x multiplier regardless of quality.
Repeating the same action type across consecutive turns yields diminishing returns.
Skip a turn to reset the fatigue counter for that action type
Fundraising, Poll, Manifesto, and Crisis Response are not subject to fatigue
Targeting the same geographic region repeatedly also sees reduced effectiveness.
Switch to a different region to reset the counter
Pairing complementary actions in the same turn on overlapping regions generates bonus effects.
Overlapping target regions (national actions overlap with any region)
Only the specific pairs listed above trigger synergies
Synergies reward concentrated effort. A Rally + Ground Game + Endorsement in one district
triggers two synergy bonuses and is far more effective than spreading those actions across three regions.
At the end of each turn, the election engine simulates the outcome based on current campaign modifiers.
Each voter calculates a utility score for every party based on:
- how close is the party to the voter's ideal position on salient issues?
- does the party leader share the voter's ethnicity?
- does the party align with the voter's religion?
- how well-known is the party in this area?
Voters also consider abstaining. A virtual 'abstention option' competes with parties. Factors making voters more likely to abstain:
Alienation: no party is close to the voter's ideal position
Indifference: multiple parties are equally appealing (no clear choice)
Demographic factors: youth, unemployment, and lower education increase abstention
Factors making voters more likely to vote:
Tertiary education, older age, urban location, public sector employment
Campaign effects: ground game, rally, patronage all reduce abstention
voting district awards seats to parties using the
There are 622 seats assigned to districts proportionally to their population
Each of the 12 campaign turns follows this sequence:
Receive 7 PC base income (plus any ETO dividends)
Hoarding cap enforced: excess above 18 PC lost
Random events may occur, applying salience shifts, valence effects, or tau modifiers
Crisis Response actions can mitigate negative effects
All actions are due by 12 AM EST on Friday of each week.
Actions are validated against your PC balance
Actions resolve in a fixed priority order
(rally, advertising, media, ethnic mob, patronage, ground game, crisis response)
Cohesion recovery (+1 natural, minus any hits)
Hoard PC above 18 (excess is lost each turn)
Use sharp manifesto shifts of >3 points (credibility + cohesion penalty)
Spam ethnic mobilization (3+ uses = 50%+ scandal chance)
Repeat the same action 3+ turns in a row (25%+ effectiveness loss)
Ignore your home base AZ for 3+ turns (cohesion penalty)
Neglect ETO early game (you'll lack income and intel late game)
